items.title: Sweep 0.4.0

items.notes: Fix build: use concrete Color in the rules-status ternary (avoid any ShapeStyle)
items.title: Sweep 0.3.5

items.notes:
Actually reopen the window on Dock click (recreate, not just front)

The v0.3.2 reopen handler only re-fronted an existing window, so after closing
the window with the red ✕ it stayed gone (you had to use the menu bar). Bridge
SwiftUI's openWindow to the AppKit reopen handler so a closed Window is properly
recreated: RootView registers `{ openWindow(id: "main") }` into WindowAccess, and
applicationShouldHandleReopen calls it (falling back to fronting an existing
window). Isolated to the window-open path; nothing else touched.
items.title: Sweep 0.3.3

items.notes:
Make duplicate deletion provably safe (byte-verify before trashing)

The duplicate finder already grouped only on full SHA-256, but deletion trusted
the scan-time result. Now every removal is proven at the moment it happens:

- Hashing.contentsEqual: streaming, hash-free byte-for-byte comparison; bails on
  first difference; any read problem returns false (never a false "equal").
- DuplicateEngine.remove: pick a surviving "keeper", and trash a candidate ONLY if
  it is byte-identical to that keeper RIGHT NOW. Files edited/replaced since the scan
  (TOCTOU) or unreadable are left untouched and reported as "skipped". The kept copy
  must still exist or the whole group is left alone. Result: it is impossible to
  trash a file that isn't a verified copy of one being kept.
- Collapse hardlinks (same device+inode) so multiple paths to one physical file
  aren't offered as independent copies that "free" space they don't.
- CleanOutcome gains `skipped` (default 0, backward compatible); FilesView surfaces
  "N left untouched (changed since the scan)".

Also: clicking the Dock icon now always reopens/fronts Sweep's window
(applicationShouldHandleReopen) instead of requiring the menu bar.
items.title: Sweep 0.3.2

items.notes:
Harden the live updater (race/UX fixes from audit)

- check(): after the network await, bail if a download/install started meanwhile
  (don't clobber a staged update); a quiet background check that fails now restores
  the prior state instead of showing a scary error or a false "up to date".
- downloadAndStage(): ignore a second trigger (double-click / both surfaces) so two
  stagings can't race the same temp dir.
- MenuBarView: show a "tap to retry" row on failure instead of going blank.
- install helper: never swap the bundle if the app hasn't actually quit.
- Updater: cancel the periodic-check task on deinit.
items.title: Sweep 0.3.1

items.notes:
Updates surface on their own: periodic auto-check + menu-bar update row

- Updater.startPeriodicChecks(): check on launch, then quietly every 6h while
  running, so a new version appears without relaunching. Skips a tick while an
  update is downloading/installing; dormant until configured; idempotent.
- AppModel.bootstrap now starts the periodic checks instead of a one-shot check.
- MenuBarView: a live "Update to X / Install & Relaunch" row in the dropdown,
  observing the updater directly — no need to open Settings or relaunch.
items.title: Sweep 0.3.0

items.notes: Set update-signing public key (activates the in-app updater)
